spatial_coverage.notes: Keck-accessible: Dec 5-35 keeps targets within airmass ~1.5 at transit from Mauna Kea (lat 19.8N). No RA constraint — sample the full SDSS spectroscopic footprint in this Dec band.

redshift.z_floor_reason: Below z=2.0, Lya (1216 A) redshifts to <3648 A — below KCWI's atmospheric blue cutoff (~3600 A)

science_goal: UV-luminous z=2-3.5 QSOs observable with KCWI at Keck (Dec 5-35, airmass<1.5) for Lyman-alpha nebula detection. Prioritize by: (1) clear FoV — no bright star V<12 within 20 arcsec, (2) u-band brightness — brighter is a stronger Lya ionizing source, (3) ancillary data richness. No known extended Lya emission in literature.
catalog_notes: Lead with SDSS DR17Q — best spectroscopic QSO catalog for this Dec range. SDSS is primary, not bonus. Rank final sample by u_mag ascending (brightest first) after quality cuts.
ranking_criteria: u_mag ascending (brightest first); secondary sort by richness of ancillary data (X-ray, radio, HST flags from SIMBAD/NED)
literature_focus: extended Lya emission, Lya nebula, Lya halo, Lyman-alpha blob, IFU spectroscopy, KCWI, CGM emission, circumgalactic medium, QSO nebula
